symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical bilateral centrofacial brown macular pigmentation (malar cheeks, forehead, upper lip)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical centrofacial distribution is the hallmark pattern of melasma.
Melanoma is characteristically a solitary asymmetric lesion, not a symmetrical bilateral field of pigmentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute darkening within 48 hours of intense UV (beach) exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: UV radiation drives melanogenesis; abrupt darkening of pre-existing melasma after sun exposure is characteristic and benign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent commencement of combined oral contraceptive pill (hormonal trigger)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Combined oral contraceptives are a recognised hormonal precipitant of melasma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Purely macular pigmentation — no scaling, induration, crusting, bleeding, ulceration or texture change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a single evolving/asymmetric/bleeding/crusting lesion is what allows melanoma to be safely downgraded on history and video.
PIH requires a preceding inflammatory dermatosis; no antecedent inflammation, scaling or texture change is present.

symptoms.symptom_name: No systemic hyperpigmentation (normal palmar creases and oral mucosa) and no constitutional symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adrenal hyperpigmentation involves palmar creases, mucosa and buccal surfaces with constitutional illness; a normal screen with pigmentation confined to sun-exposed skin excludes it clinically.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin texture and induration on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable induration/nodularity would raise concern for an invasive lesion — a clinician's hands-on finding, sealed and unavailable over video; drives the recommendation for in-person review if any single lesion changes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dermoscopic pattern of a pigmented lesion

investigation_details.expected_result: A regular pigment network consistent with benign melasma rather than melanoma-specific criteria — cannot be assessed remotely; only indicated if a single lesion later evolves.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Not required for classic symmetrical melasma; would only be needed if a solitary changing lesion appeared.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dermoscopy ± biopsy is the definitive tool to exclude melanoma in a suspicious lesion — a clinician/equipment task, sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe acute health anxiety with panic attacks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The distress is the presenting driver but does not discriminate the dermatological diagnosis; it is the key management target for de-escalation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Symmetrical centrofacial macular pigmentation + UV trigger + hormonal (OCP) trigger + purely macular (no texture change) together constitute a high-specificity benign melasma constellation, and simultaneously argue against a solitary melanoma.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin biopsy of the pigmented area
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Biopsy is NOT indicated for classic symmetrical macular melasma and can itself provoke post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation; reserve for a single lesion with melanoma features on later in-person review.

Not required — the clinical adrenal screen is negative (well patient, normal palms/mucosa, sun-confined pigmentation). Ordering bloods here would be over-investigation prompted by the red-herring framing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person dermatology / skin-cancer skin check with dermoscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Routine, safety-net only — appropriate if any single lesion later grows, becomes asymmetric, bleeds or crusts; not urgent for the current benign symmetrical pattern.
